title: Exclude .takt/beads/ from feature branch worktrees to eliminate merge conflicts
description:
After git worktree add, configure the new worktree so .takt/beads/ is never tracked on the feature branch. Bead state should only ever be committed on main.

Two changes required:

1. In src/agent_takt/gitutils.py, add a helper _write_worktree_exclude(repo_root, worktree_path) that writes .takt/beads/ to the per-worktree exclude file at repo_root/.git/worktrees/<worktree_name>/info/exclude (creating the directory if needed). Call this immediately after the git worktree add subprocess call in the worktree creation function.

2. After writing the exclude file, run:
   git -C <worktree_path> rm -r --cached --ignore-unmatch .takt/beads/
   git -C <worktree_path> commit -m 'chore: untrack bead state from feature branch [skip ci]' --allow-empty
   This removes already-checked-out bead files from the index without deleting them from disk. Use --allow-empty in case there are no bead files yet.

3. Add the following line to .gitattributes (create the file if it does not exist):
   .takt/beads/** merge=ours
   This is a belt-and-suspenders measure so any path that bypasses the exclude file still cannot create conflicts.

No changes to storage.py, scheduler logic, or any other file. Only gitutils.py and .gitattributes are in scope.
